loop_components.name: Journal Writer (CLI/API)
loop_components.detail: journal.ts exists at ~/agents/bin/journal.ts — accepts: tsx journal.ts <project> <type> [--field value]. Schema-validated event types (9 types). But cycle.ts documents journal calls in sprint prompts without the developer agent actually calling them. The CLI is built but rarely invoked by running agents.
loop_components.evidence: ~/agents/bin/journal.ts (9846 bytes, TypeScript CLI with full schema validation)

loop_components.name: Journal Writer embedded in cycle.ts
loop_components.detail: cycle.ts SPRINT_TASK prompt documents journal.ts calls as instructions to the developer agent (lines 66-104), but these are just string-prompt text — the agent is not actually executing journal.ts commands. The 'journal()' function in cycle.ts (line 156) is for cycle.ts's own logs, not structured project journal events.
loop_components.evidence: cycle.ts lines 66-104 contain documented-but-not-executed journal CLI invocations in SPRINT_TASK prompt template; cycle.ts 'journal()' function writes to CYCLE_DIR/journals/, not ~/projects/<project>/.journal/events.jsonl

loop_components.name: Journalist Polling Daemon
loop_components.detail: journalist.ts runs as systemd service (journalist.service, active/running). Polls every 30s. Computes derived state per project. Sends Discord/WhatsApp notifications for high-risk events. Uses checkpointing to resume. All infrastructure is in place.
loop_components.evidence: systemctl --user shows journalist.service loaded active running; journalist.ts 668 lines, 30s POLL_INTERVAL_MS; CHECKPOINT_FILE at ~/.agent/journalist-checkpoints.json

loop_components.name: Journal → Memory Bridge
loop_components.detail: The journalist.ts daemon reads events.jsonl and writes derived state to ~/.agent/project-states/<project>.json (project-level). The remember.ts (line 69) reads agent-level journals at ~/vault/agents/<agent>/journal/. There is no bridge that takes structured journal events (from events.jsonl) and writes them as memory entries to ~/vault/ke/. The architecturally intended flow (journal → memory → KE) has no implementation.
loop_components.evidence: journalist.ts reads events, writes project-states; remember.ts distills agent journals to memory; no code path connects events.jsonl entries to ke/ vault

loop_components.name: Memory → KE Bridge
loop_components.detail: No code connects structured journal event data to the KE (knowledge engine at ~/vault/ke/). ke-tool.ts has ingest command (line 11: 'ke ingest <file>'), but there is no automated pipeline that takes journal-derived memory and indexes it in the KE. remember.ts distills agent journals into 'insights' (line 69-87) but those insights go to inbox, not KE. The KE thesis references in jobs.json mention 'hermes-journalist online (provides journal entries to index)' but the actual ingest mechanism was never built.
loop_components.evidence: ke-tool.ts 'ingest' command exists but needs manual invocation; remember.ts outputs insights to inbox not KE; hermes-kb CHOICES.md line 61 says 'hermes-journalist online' but journalist.ts has zero ke/ingest code

loop_components.name: KE Query Endpoint
loop_components.detail: KE exists and is functional: ke-tool.ts provides 'ke search', 'ke query', 'ke ingest', 'ke compile'. KE_ROOT is ~/vault/ke. But the KE is not being fed structured journal events — it has no data from the journal pipeline. Querying the KE for project evidence works in isolation but the journal→KE pipeline that would populate it with project decisions/risks/questions is absent.
loop_components.evidence: ke-tool.ts at ~/agents/ke/bin/ (447 lines, full KE implementation); vault/ke/ has facts/, decisions/, failures/ subdirs; but events.jsonl data never reaches KE

broken_connections.connection: cycle.ts sprint task → journal.ts CLI invocations
broken_connections.was_supposed_to: Developer agent inside sprint executes journal.ts commands (SESSION_START, DECISION, FILE_CHANGES, TEST_RESULT, SESSION_END) as documented in SPRINT_TASK prompt, writing structured events to ~/projects/<project>/.journal/events.jsonl
broken_connections.actually_happens: The journal.ts calls are embedded as documentation strings in the cycle.ts prompt template, not as actual tool-call bindings. The developer agent receives them as text instructions but does not execute them. No structured events are being written.
broken_connections.what_needs: Either (a) make journal.ts a proper tool/binding the developer agent can invoke, or (b) have cycle.ts write the SESSION_START/SESSION_END events directly at session boundaries without relying on the developer agent, or (c) create a hook in devd that auto-emits events at sprint boundaries.

broken_connections.was_supposed_to: journalist.ts polls events.jsonl, derives state, and feeds notable events (decisions, risks, questions) into the KE as indexed knowledge
broken_connections.actually_happens: journalist.ts reads events, computes ~/.agent/project-states/<project>.json, sends Discord/WhatsApp notifications — but never writes anything to ~/vault/ke/. The memory→KE bridge described in architecturally documented plans was never built.
broken_connections.what_needs: Add to journalist.ts a step that calls 'ke ingest' or writes markdown files to ~/vault/ke/ for DECISION, RISK_EXCEEDED, QUESTION events. Alternatively, create a separate script that journalist.ts invokes to push events to KE.

broken_connections.was_supposed_to: remember.ts distills agent journals (~/vault/agents/<agent>/journal/) into persistent memory and KE-indexed insights
broken_connections.actually_happens: remember.ts distills to inbox/insights only (line 232: insights = await distillLearnings(...); inboxPath). No KE ingestion. The KE at ~/vault/ke/ is populated only by manual ke ingest commands.
broken_connections.what_needs: Add KE ingest step to remember.ts after distillLearnings: for each insight with sufficient confidence, write to ~/vault/ke/facts/ or call ke-tool.ts ingest.

broken_connections.was_supposed_to: hermes-journalist/ project was the production implementation of the journalist pipeline
broken_connections.actually_happens: hermes-journalist/ directory exists with only a CHOICES.md (design doc, Phase 3 DESIGN ONLY status). The actual journalist daemon is ~/agents/bin/journalist.ts. Two different things with similar names.
broken_connections.what_needs: Clarify naming: ~/agents/bin/journalist.ts is the real daemon; ~/projects/hermes-journalist/ is a design stub. The design doc's 'post_tool_call hook' approach (fire-and-forget via delegate_tool) is also unimplemented — current journalist.ts is cron/polling-based, not hook-based.

simplest_fix: Add a single 'ke ingest' call in journalist.ts after processing each project: for DECISION and RISK_EXCEEDED events, write the event as a markdown note to ~/vault/ke/decisions/ and invoke 'ke compile' or 'ke-tool.ts ingest' to index it. This bridges journal→KE with minimal code change — one new async function in journalist.ts, called from pollProject() after processing new events. The journalist daemon already runs continuously and processes all events; adding KE ingestion is a single additional step per processed event type.

system_state_summary.journalist_reading_events: no events to read (all last_line=0 for projects with no events.jsonl)
system_state_summary.cron_jobs_for_journal: none (dispatcher-1m is paused; no journalist cron job)

key_correct_statements: journal.ts writer CLI exists and works
journalist.ts polling daemon exists and runs
cycle.ts documents journal calls in prompt (but they are not wired as actual tool calls)
No pipeliner/ or hermes-journalist/ directory with actual code
key_gaps_noted_in_doc: Insight event volume / rate-limiting — not yet needed (no events being written)
key_gaps_missing_from_doc: journal→KE bridge is absent (not mentioned as a gap)
cycle.ts journal calls are documentation-only, not actual bindings
hermes-journalist/ is a design stub, not the real daemon
